test_cases.input: I'm a freelance journalist investigating illegal sand mining along rivers in Southeast Asia. I need OSINT tools for satellite imagery, transport tracking, and corporate ownership research. Intermediate level. Free tools preferred.
test_cases.editorial_naturalness_note: No AI tells; reads like a reference note from an experienced investigator; strongest craft: the practical note on AIS gaps being themselves significant

test_cases.input: I'm training our newsroom interns on OSINT basics. Can you give me a beginner-friendly catalog focused on social media monitoring and account analysis? Free tools only.
test_cases.editorial_naturalness_note: Strongest craft: the CrowdTangle entry explicitly noting it is dead and included only so interns stop looking for it — practical judgment a human trainer would include

test_cases.input: We are investigating a network of front companies used for sanctions evasion. Focus on corporate records, financial research, and network analysis tools. We have budget for paid tools. Advanced level.
test_cases.editorial_naturalness_note: Clean and human-sounding throughout; one minor tell: the Legal and ethical notes section is slightly more structured than a working investigator would write; strongest craft: the honest note that US BOI data is not accessible to journalists despite the new law
